summary: ollama-helpers postinstall exfiltrates developer git/GitHub identity to attacker-controlled Cloud Run endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js reads installer-owned identity files (~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,.git/config, ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml) and parses the `[user] email` and gh `user`/`email` fields. It bundles the extracted git email and GitHub login together with os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), OS platform, CI-env detection, and a timestamp into a JSON body, then POSTs it via https.request to npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app (a Google Cloud Run endpoint unrelated to the package's claimed homepage ollama-js.dev). Comments in the script falsely describe the beacon as 'anonymous telemetry' with 'no credentials transmitted', while the code transmits exactly that developer identity data. The package name, author, homepage, and repository all impersonate the legitimate `ollama` ecosystem to maximize installs. Every developer who installs this package leaks their git email, GitHub username, machine hostname, OS username, and project working directory to the operator of the Cloud Run endpoint, enabling targeted phishing and reconnaissance of corporate developer environments.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
